reasoning: The resolution criteria require between 10 (inclusive) and 12 (inclusive) SpaceX Starship launches to successfully reach an altitude of 62 miles between January 1, 2025, and December 31, 2025. Based on recent information up to November 2025, SpaceX has only conducted five Starship test flights in 2025 (Flights 7 through 11). Even if all five reached an altitude of 62 miles, five is the maximum number of successful launches achieved so far in the year. With just one or two months remaining in 2025, the cadence (currently ~1.5 to 3 months between launches) cannot physically accommodate 5 to 7 additional launches by December 31, 2025. The next flight is projected to be about four weeks away from October (around November). Thus, it is virtually impossible to reach the 10-12 threshold. I assign a probability of 0.02 (the lowest allowed limit).
